title: Managing portfolios | SonarQube Cloud Documentation
content:
# Managing portfolios

## [Permissions](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/managing-portfolios/#permissions "Permissions")

To manage a portfolio, you will need at least Edit permissions granted by the portfolio’s administrator. See the [Administering portfolios](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/getting-started-with-enterprise/administering-portfolios/ "Administering portfolios") article for details.

## [Creating a portfolio](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/managing-portfolios/#creating-portfolio "Creating a portfolio")

You can create a portfolio if you have the **Create Portfolio** permission enabled.

![](https://docs.sonarsource.com/Base64-Image-Removed)

![Create portfolio button](https://assets-eu-01.kc-usercontent.com:443/b1eeb429-d9e0-0100-be87-468f6802040a/6e9b25d7-d74c-4bcb-bde2-7de23522951a/create-portfolio.png?w=1399&h=735&auto=format&fit=crop "Create portfolio button")

1. Click **My Portfolios** in the top navigation bar and select the enterprise to which you want to add a new portfolio.

2. Click **Create portfolio** on the Portfolios home page to start the portfolio creation wizard.

You can also create a portfolio by clicking the ‘+’ sign at the top right of the page.

![](https://docs.sonarsource.com/Base64-Image-Removed)

![Portfolio creation wizard](https://assets-eu-01.kc-usercontent.com:443/b1eeb429-d9e0-0100-be87-468f6802040a/fd0931b0-dd10-47eb-a6f4-03ae3774f584/portfolio-creation-wizard.png?w=1400&h=1010&auto=format&fit=crop "Portfolio creation wizard")

The wizard takes you through 4 steps of the portfolio creation process and the system automatically saves the information you have entered when you go to the next step. You can find the draft portfolios on the Portfolios home page and resume the process in case you get interrupted.

The portfolio creation process consists of the following steps:

1. **Add details**: Choose the enterprise where the portfolio will reside if you have permission to more than one enterprise. Then, enter the portfolio name and description.

2. **Add projects**: From a list of organizations and projects to which you have permissions, select projects to include in the portfolio.

3. **Set permissions**: Add portfolio Administer, Edit, and View permissions to specific groups and users or apply a permission template.

4. **Review**: Take a final look at all the portfolio details and click **Complete** to finalize the process.

To ensure a reliable security report, check that the relevant security rules are activated in your quality profiles for projects you have included in your portfolio. For instance, if no rule corresponding to a given OWASP category is activated in your quality profile, you won't get Security issues or Security Hotspots linked to that specific category in the OWASP report. See Checking the security rules included in a project’s quality profile article for more information.

Once the portfolio is created, it will be populated with ratings for Releasability, Security, Reliability, Maintainability, and Security Review.

## [Editing a portfolio](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/managing-portfolios/#editing-portfolio "Editing a portfolio")

With the Edit permission on a portfolio, you can add and remove projects from it. Note that you don't add a project, but a long-lived branch of a project. Currently, you can only add a single branch per project.

![](https://docs.sonarsource.com/Base64-Image-Removed)

![Portfolio definition](https://assets-eu-01.kc-usercontent.com:443/b1eeb429-d9e0-0100-be87-468f6802040a/e09f4292-dc6e-49e2-9d79-26f69614814c/edit-portfolio.png?w=1400&h=645&auto=format&fit=crop "Portfolio definition")

Proceed as follows:

1. In the top navigation bar, go to **My Portfolios** and select your enterprise from the drop-down menu.

2. On the Portfolios home page, select the portfolio you want to edit.

3. Go to **Settings** > **Portfolio Definition**.

4. Select **Edit selection**.

5. From a list of organizations and projects to which you have permissions, select projects to include or exclude from the portfolio. Alternatively, use the search field to find projects by name.

6. If the selected project contains several long-lived branches, select the branch to be added. By default, the **main** branch is selected.

7. Click **Save**.

## [Deleting a portfolio](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/managing-portfolios/#deleting-portfolio "Deleting a portfolio")

With the Edit permission on a portfolio, you can remove it from the system.

1. In the top navigation bar, go to **My Portfolios** and select your enterprise from the drop-down menu.

2. Select the portfolio you want to remove.

3. Go to **Settings** > **Delete** portfolio.

## [Portfolio recomputation](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/managing-portfolios/#portfolio-recomputation "Portfolio recomputation")

The following events will trigger the recalculation of a portfolio:

- When a project within the portfolio has a new analysis.
- If a project is removed from an organization within the enterprise.
- When an organization is removed from the enterprise.

For information on how ratings are calculated, see [Understanding measures and metrics](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-cloud/digging-deeper/metric-definitions/ "Understanding measures and metrics") article.
